page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dlsv7 family, powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but supports Premium Storage configurations.

The server demonstrates strong multi-core and computational capabilities. In benchmarks, it achieves top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and single-threaded gzip compression also register in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 333.72 GB/sec for large-block read operations. For specialized workloads, it delivers high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving, alongside top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM inference models.

Due to its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2, the D96ls_v7 is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The absence of local storage means users must rely on external network storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is optimized for high-compute tasks such as web serving, compression and decompression pipelines, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-focused server from the Dlsv7 family, featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 192.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. It excels in multi-core processing, achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and high throughput for Redis operations and static web serving. Given its resource density, the D96ls_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive workloads that do not demand high memory capacity. It is well-suited for web serving, data compression pipelines, cryptographic tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high throughput for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute server featuring ninety-six virtual cores and optimized memory allocation for intensive processing and high-throughput workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and cryptography
No local storage bundled, requiring external Premium Storage
Suited for web serving, compression pipelines, and CPU-based inference
